medications.name: Metformin
medications.indication: Type 2 Diabetes — reduces hepatic glucose production

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.caution: Interaction with Simvastatin — limit statin to 20mg

medications.name: Simvastatin
medications.caution: Myopathy risk when combined with Amlodipine

visits.clinicalNote: Patient presents with polyuria and fatigue. HbA1c elevated at 7.2%. Counselled extensively on diabetic diet — restrict refined carbohydrates, increase fibre. Increased Metformin to 1000mg twice daily. BP noted at 142/88 — monitoring advised. Lipid profile shows elevated LDL 142. Started Simvastatin 20mg. Patient is a retired government employee, moderate activity level.
visits.plan: Repeat HbA1c in 3 months. Lipid recheck in 6 months. Diet counselling referral.

visits.clinicalNote: BP poorly controlled at 150/94 despite lifestyle measures. HbA1c 8.1% — worsening trend noted. Added Amlodipine 5mg once daily for BP. Patient reports occasional dizziness especially on standing — likely postural hypotension. No chest pain. ECG done — normal sinus rhythm. Creatinine 1.3 — borderline, monitoring nephropathy. Counselled on sodium restriction.
visits.plan: Review BP in 4 weeks. Repeat creatinine in 3 months. Continue Metformin + Simvastatin.

visits.clinicalNote: HbA1c 9.0% — significant worsening over 6 consecutive visits. Creatinine rising to 1.9 mg/dL, eGFR dropping — early nephropathy progressing. Referred for nephrology consult urgently. Patient reluctant to change diet. Denies allergy to penicillin but reports rash with amoxicillin in 2019 — DOCUMENT: Beta-lactam antibiotic caution. Amlodipine dose maintained. Considering insulin initiation at next visit.
visits.plan: Nephrology referral. Urine microalbumin test. Insulin counselling at next visit.

visits.clinicalNote: Patient transferred from Dr. Nandakumar. HbA1c 9.4 — insulin initiation being actively considered. Creatinine 2.1 concerning — nephropathy well established. BP target not met despite Amlodipine — considering dose escalation or addition of ARB. Cardiology referral initiated for cardiovascular risk assessment. Drug interaction noted: Amlodipine + Simvastatin — patient counselled. Beta-lactam allergy documented prominently.
visits.plan: Initiate insulin Glargine 10 units at night. Cardiology referral. Nephrology follow-up. Repeat labs in 6 weeks.

clinicalFlags.recommendation: Insulin initiation being considered — for physician review
Consider dose escalation or ARB addition — for physician review

clinicalFlags.evidence: eGFR dropped from 72 to 51 mL/min. Creatinine 2.1 mg/dL
clinicalFlags.recommendation: Nephrology referral. Avoid nephrotoxic drugs — for physician review
Monitor for myopathy symptoms. Limit Simvastatin to 20mg — for physician review
Avoid penicillin-class antibiotics — for physician review
